summary: Requests is a simple, yet elegant Python HTTP library that allows developers to send HTTP/1.1 requests extremely easily. It provides an intuitive API for making HTTP requests without manually adding query strings to URLs or form-encoding POST/PUT data. The library supports features like Keep-Alive & Connection Pooling, International Domains and URLs, Sessions with Cookie Persistence, TLS/SSL Verification, Basic & Digest Authentication, Automatic Content Decompression, Multi-part File Uploads, SOCKS Proxy Support, Connection Timeouts, and Streaming Downloads. It is one of the most downloaded Python packages, with approximately 30 million downloads per week.
structure: The project follows a standard Python package structure with the main source code located in `src/requests/`. The core modules include: `api.py` (high-level API functions), `sessions.py` (session management), `adapters.py` (HTTP adapter implementations), `models.py` (request/response models), `auth.py` (authentication handling), `cookies.py` (cookie management), `utils.py` (utility functions), `exceptions.py` (custom exceptions), `hooks.py` (hook system), `structures.py` (data structures), `compat.py` (compatibility layer), `certs.py` (certificate handling), `status_codes.py` (HTTP status codes), `help.py` (help utilities), and `packages.py` ( vendored packages management). The `tests/` directory contains comprehensive test suites including certificate configurations for testing TLS/SSL scenarios (expired, mtls, valid certificates). Documentation is built with Sphinx and located in `docs/`. Configuration is managed via `pyproject.toml` and `setup.py`.
